content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

November 16, 1995


Thom Stark
Stark Realities
P.O. Box 457
El Cerrito, CA 94530-04576

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-95-318

Dear Mr. Stark:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Since your
question, in part, involves past conduct, we are rendering you
informal assistance consistent with the provisions of regulation
18329(c).2/

QUESTION

You are a candidate for the El Cerrito City Council. You
have created a virtual campaign headquarters on the Internet World
Wide Web. On one of its pages you outline the conditions under
which a would-be contributor can avoid having his or her name
listed in your campaign disclosure statement (i.e., by
contributing less than $100 total for the calendar year).

One of your opponents has been attempting to make a campaign
issue of the instructions on the Web. He has repeatedly claimed
that your publication of these facts constitutes a major breach of
campaign ethics.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

No. I-95-318
Page 2


You have asked whether the information contained in your
materials constitutes a breach of campaign ethics.

CONCLUSION

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit you from outlining
the provisions of Section 84211(f), which requires candidates and
committees to disclose the name, address, occupation and employer
of persons from whom they have received contributions totaling
$100 or more during a calendar year. Since the Commission has
jurisdiction only with regard to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act, we offer no comment on whether your publication of the
materials constitutes a breach of campaign ethics.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[illegible] Mayer
By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
